pattern: Algebraic simplification collapses a four-term absolute-value test into a single magnitude ratio, so sorting by absolute value lets a two-pointer count the qualifying pairs in linear time.
hints: Try rewriting min and max of |a-b| and |a+b| purely in terms of |a| and |b|. The two expressions are the difference and the sum of magnitudes.
Once simplified, one of the two conditions is always satisfied, and the other reduces to comparing the larger magnitude against twice the smaller one.
Sort the values by absolute value; for each element as the larger one, the valid partners form a contiguous window, which a sliding two-pointer counts without rechecking pairs.

edge_inputs.description: magnitudes too far apart, no pairs
minimum length single pair that qualifies
negative versus positive of equal magnitude
exact boundary where larger equals twice smaller

stress.description: random mixed-sign values near a common scale so many pairs qualify
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randrange(-50, 51) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
